content: Good morning, team. Reminder that the Joint Commission walkthrough is scheduled for February 9. Please ensure all pastoral care documentation is current.
Noted. I'll pull together the CPE supervision logs before end of week. Residents are on track.
I'm taking overnight on-call this Thursday (January 22). If anything changes let me know by Wednesday noon.
Confirmed. I have Monday the 22nd. Residents will shadow me on that shift - good learning opportunity.

Great reflective writing this week, both of you. For next Tuesday's supervision session, come with one moment from your oncology visits where you felt pulled to fix rather than witness. We'll work through that together.
Thank you, Rebecca. I have exactly one of those. Looking forward to Tuesday.
All staff: the new palliative care consult form goes live February 1. Training session is January 26, 10 AM, Conference Room B. Attendance required.
Rebecca, audio board is booked for your 5:30 setup Saturday. Engineer says the mic pack is fully charged and ready.
Perfect. Theme this Saturday is 'Staying Rooted When the Ground Shifts.' Guest is confirmed. Rundown is in the Segment Planning channel.

January 17 rundown draft: 7:00 open music (Lauren Daigle), 7:08 welcome + theme intro, 7:15 guest Rev. Angela Dawson (Golden Heart Medical), 7:45 listener call-in, 8:20 closing meditation, 8:45 sponsor reads (Covenant Insurance, Mitchell's Flowers, Bluff City Auto Care), 9:00 close.
Confirmed. Covenant Insurance approved the new read copy for the new season. I'll drop it in Sponsors channel.

Covenant Insurance seasonal read copy attached. Mitchell's Flowers invoice for December was processed. Bluff City Auto Care renewing through December - TJ to handle contract, Rebecca just needs to keep the 30-second read in rotation.
Rebecca, your meditation at the early-morning service was exactly what the congregation needed. Several members asked if the text would be shared. Your call.
Thank you, Pastor Greer. I'll clean it up and share it in the bulletin insert for next Sunday. Give me a couple days.
Fall festival trunk-or-treat is October 31. Rebecca, you're confirmed as emcee again. Planning meeting January 25 after service.
On the calendar. I'll block January 25 and October 31. Same format as last year - costume parade, scripture reading, then trunk distribution?
Community health fair volunteers: we need 8 people for the blood pressure and vision screening stations. Sandra Coles confirmed to lead the nursing education table. Can we lock the date as October 3?
October 3 works. I'll post the volunteer sign-up in the Sunday bulletin. Glad Sandra is on board - she always brings her nursing students and that doubles our capacity.

CE hours update as of January 16: I'm at 28 of the 50 required for 2026. Upcoming: APC webinar February 6 (3 hrs), Memphis Interfaith Chaplaincy Summit March 14 (6 hrs). That gets me to 37. Still need 13 more by Dec 31.

Rebecca, the joint grief support group pilot between Bluff City Medical and Golden Heart went really well last month. Administration at my end wants to formalize it quarterly. Can we meet before the March summit to draft a proposal?

Yes. Let's aim for the week of February 2 - my calendar is lighter then. A quarterly inter-hospital grief group is exactly the kind of thing both departments need. I'll loop in Dr. Vickers once we have a draft.
